summary: Decompiled and typed more of player attack-stat tracking and fighter device collision/wind callback plumbing. The PR renamed Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr to Player_GetActionStats, replaced the opaque pl_800386D8_t with plActionStats/plAttackStats, added a StatsAttack enum, implemented most of plAttack_8003759C as a reset routine, and improved ftDevice structures/callback signatures to use Ground_GObj and Fighter_GObj instead of generic UNK_T/int casts. It also propagated the new names through plbonus, plbonuslib, pltrick, Fox appeal checks, ftcoll wind offset logic, and stage callers.

key_files.role: Added plStats_Attack enum and renamed forward declaration from pl_800386D8_t to plActionStats.
Replaced opaque pl_800386D8_t layout with plAttackStats and plActionStats, including attacks/hits/x358_hits fields and documented uncertain union layout.
Implemented plAttack_8003759C reset logic for action stats, contributing the largest matching improvement.
Renamed Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr to Player_GetActionStats while still returning StaticPlayer.stale_moves.total_attack_count_struct.
Updated bonus logic to use plActionStats field names and StatsAttack enum values instead of raw offsets and hex indices.
Renamed ftDeviceUnk3 fields from x0/x4 to ground/type.
Changed ftDevice_Callback0 signature to bool (*)(Ground_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, Vec3*) and included gr/forward.h.
Improved ftCo_800C07F8 typing and matching, using ft_80459A68[3]/ft_80459A8C knowledge and preserving TODOs for uncertain callback type.
Updated wind offset loop to use typed ground object, fighter object, type field, and typed active_cb invocation.
Updated symbol name at 0x800361D4 from Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr to Player_GetActionStats.

what_changed: Renamed the player accessor at 0x800361D4 from Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr to Player_GetActionStats in symbols, player.c, player.h, and call sites.
Renamed pl_800386D8_t to plActionStats and split part of its internal layout into reusable plAttackStats blocks for attacks, hits, and likely x358_hits.
Added plStats_Attack enum values from StatsAttack_None through StatsAttack_Count, replacing many raw attack-stat indices such as 0x33, 0x34, 0x35, 0x39, and 0x3E with named constants.
Implemented plAttack_8003759C(u32 slot), clearing per-attack counts, totals, item/aerial/special counters, x504 bonus-related bytes, x568-x594 counters, x598 entries, and x5BC bitfields.
Updated pl_800386D8 to take plActionStats* and read by_attack_hi[arg1] instead of the old x3E8 array name.
Updated plbonus and plbonuslib to use names like attacks.by_attack_counts, hits.by_attack_counts, attacks.total, and StatsAttack_Catch/ThrowF/CargoThrowF/LedgeAttackSlow.
Updated pltrick and Fox side-taunt appeal code to consume plActionStats and Player_GetActionStats.
Typed ftDevice_Callback0 as a callback from Ground_GObj plus Fighter_GObj plus Vec3 output, instead of UNK_T/HSD_GObj.
Renamed ftDeviceUnk3 fields x0 and x4 to ground and type; ftcoll now iterates ft_80459A68[i].ground and calls active_cb(grp, fgp, &wind).
Changed ftCo_800C07F8 signature from generic UNK_T/int/int to Ground_GObj*, u32, void*, removed stage-side integer casts for callbacks, and made ft_80459A8C an ftDeviceUnk3 extern.
smart_moves: Introduced plStats_Attack before refactoring bonus code, making decomp output more self-documenting and reducing fragile magic-number logic around catches, throws, and ledge attacks.
Kept uncertain structure knowledge explicit: plActionStats contains a TODO explaining why x358_hits is modeled as a union and why the x3E8/by_attack_hi split is suspicious.
Improved typing where evidence was clear but retained void* for ftCo_800C07F8's callback parameter with a TODO, avoiding overclaiming that every callback is always ftDevice_Callback0.
Used field renames in ftDeviceUnk3 to unlock clearer ftcoll code: ground/type/active_cb communicate the device collision table's purpose better than x0/x4.
Changed stage call sites to pass callback function pointers directly instead of casting them through int, improving type safety without changing behavior.
The reset implementation for plAttack_8003759C follows the discovered struct layout directly, clearing each repeated plAttackStats-like block consistently.
decomp_lessons: When a function name describes only one field, revisit the surrounding struct: Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr actually exposed a larger action-stat block, so Player_GetActionStats was a better name.
Replacing hex indices with enums can make bonus-condition code much easier to audit while still preserving exact numeric values via commented enum ordinals.
A partial struct split is useful even when not all fields are understood; plAttackStats clarified repeated total/by_attack/counter patterns while x568-x594 stayed conservative.
Keep TODOs near uncertain ABI or layout decisions. This PR documents uncertainty around ftDevice callbacks and the plActionStats x358/x3E8 layout instead of hiding it behind confident names.
Use forward headers to break include cycles when improving pointer types; ft/forward.h gained gr/forward.h so callback typedefs could mention Ground_GObj.
Renaming a symbol should be propagated through config/GALE01/symbols.txt, headers, implementation, and all call sites in the same PR to keep the repository searchable and coherent.
naming_conventions: Use Player_GetActionStats for accessors returning the full player action-stat structure, not Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr.
Use plActionStats for the overall player action-stat container formerly named pl_800386D8_t.
Use plAttackStats for repeated blocks containing total, by_attack_counts, thrown_item_count, aerials_count, specials_count, and related counters.
Use StatsAttack_* enum names for attack-stat IDs, preserving ordinal comments such as /* 33 */ StatsAttack_Catch.
Use descriptive ftDeviceUnk3 fields ground and type instead of x0 and x4 when referring to registered fighter device collision entries.
Use ftDevice_Callback0 for active callbacks with signature Ground_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, Vec3* when evidence supports that callback shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented plAttack_8003759C as explicit loops over StatsAttack_Count ranges and explicit scalar clears, raising automated match from 0.00% to 89.04%.
For the attack-stat reset, loops begin at index 2 for per-attack arrays and use StatsAttack_Count as the bound, matching the discovered behavior while avoiding raw 0x64 constants.

ftCo_800C07F8 matching improved by recognizing the target slot as ft_80459A68[3] / ft_80459A8C, checking ground == NULL, assigning ground/type/active_cb, incrementing ft_804D6570, and using the original OSReport/assert failure path.
ftcoll wind offset matching benefited from typing ft_80459A68 entries as Ground_GObj* plus u32 type, then passing the fighter gobj through ftCo_800C0A28 and active_cb.
The automated decomp.dev report showed ftCo_800C07F8 improved from 83.77% to 87.81%, indicating that type/field cleanup affected codegen but did not fully solve matching.

review_feedback.feedback: Automated report for GALE01 showed 2 unmatched-function improvements: plAttack_8003759C improved by 1235 bytes from 0.00% to 89.04%, and ftCo_800C07F8 improved by 5 bytes from 83.77% to 87.81%.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve the TODO about whether ftCo_800C07F8 arg2 is always ftDevice_Callback0.
Find later PRs that refine plActionStats x358_hits/by_attack_hi union layout or rename x568-x594 fields.
Search for remaining Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr references after PR 1990.
Search for remaining raw attack-stat IDs that could be replaced by StatsAttack_* enum names.
Investigate why plAttack_8003759C remained at 89.04% and what remaining codegen/matching issue blocked full match.
